data.narrative:
AnySignal is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $34M+. Valuation analysis relies on a comp universe of five public-traded space companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers spanning the sector, including ClearSpace, Planet Labs, and Rocket Lab.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $34M raised proxy, implying a $68M valuation. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B)—yields a median of $400M, with a range from $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, a standard limitation for private companies. The sector-wide public P/S median stands at 45.3x across six tickers.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $58M to $460M, with a median of $234M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound; peer medians anchor the upper end. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, competitive positioning within the satellite/space infrastructure segment, and burn rate sustainability through Series B. Resolution of these factors would refine the midpoint estimate materially.
